info.title: Careem POS API
info.description: Careem POS API enables partners to provide restaurants a convenient way to manage Careem orders as part of their regular operations. You may download the OpenAPI specification here. # Overview This section discusses challenges that arise from manual integration, followed by an overview of Careem's POS API and its advantages. ## Challenges with manual integration Manual integration with POS systems is known to be associated with many problems. Some of the common problems have been highlighted below: - Punching data manually is **error-prone**. This can cause data inconsistencies in the POS system, leading to order cancellations. - Punching data manually is **time-consuming**. This slows down order processing and impacts customer satisfaction directly. - Punching data manually can be extremely **demanding** during high volumes of orders, leading to burnout. - Punching data manually is **unscalable**. This can make it challenging for businesses to scale their operations. ## POS API overview Careem offers its own integration API, which partners can use to seamlessly integrate with their POS systems. The **Store API** allows partners to create and manage their stores on Careem's platform. Store management includes the following key features: - Enable/disable POS integration - Mark the store online/offline - Update operational hours The **Catalog API** allows partners to create and manage catalogs for their stores. The API also includes sending catalog sync notifications to a partner's webhook. Catalog management includes the following key features: - Support partial catalogs - Track catalog sync status - Reset catalog - Update item availability The **Order API** allows partners to retrieve and manage orders. The API also includes sending order notifications to a partner's webhook. Order management includes the following key features: - Accept/cancel orders - Mark order as ready - Tag orders - Receive order notifications via webhook ## Benefits With Careem's integration API, partners can take advantage of the following benefits: - Increased order accuracy - Increased operational excellence - Enhanced customer experience - Improved order fulfillment KPIs # Getting started This section serves as a starting point for using our API. ## Pre-requisites Before you begin to use our APIs, please ensure: - Completion of contract and submission of essential legal and regulatory compliance documentation as per instructions by our commercial team. If you've not done so already, please feel free to reach out to them. - Received approval from our information security & legal teams. Furthermore, it is also necessary to complete the following tasks: ### Webhook configuration In order to receive catalog sync and order notifications from our platform, you need to provide the following information: - **A valid webhook URL:** This URL must be a secure HTTPS URL that is capable of receiving requests via the POST method, as this is the method our API uses to send notifications to your system. - **An API key secret value:** Our API will send this key to your system as part of every API request, and it will be included in a header called `x-careem-api-key`. This will allow you to confirm that the notification is coming from our API and that it has been authenticated. #### Example of the webhook request header ```text x-careem-api-key: a12345f-1337-4bff-8e6b-6b38a221322f ``` **Note: Your service must have a valid SSL certificate and be accessible via the standard SSL port (port 443).** ## Integration Process Overview ```mermaid flowchart TD A[Timeline agreement] --> B[Setup client] B --> C[Setup test brand] C --> D[Setup test branch] D --> E[Map the test branch] E --> F[Push catalog] F --> G[Share SuperApp APK] G --> H[Provide ongoing support] H --> I[Perform end-to-end testing with QA] I --> J[Production rollout] ``` ### Timeline agreement We require due dates from our partners for completion of their development work so we can make a project plan accordingly. ### Setup client We would set up an OAuth client on our end and share its credentials with you. ### Setup test brand The partner would create a test brand via the brand API. ### Setup test branch The partner would create a test branch (linked to the test brand) via the branch API. Additionally, the partner would also enable POS integration for the test branch (also via the branch API). ### Map the test branch Upon creation of the test branch, the partner would request the operations team to map the test branch to an outlet on our platform. ### Push catalog The partner would push a catalog for the test branch via the catalog API. ### Share SuperApp APK The engineering team would share an APK for the SuperApp so that the partner can validate their pushed catalog and also perform their own testing. ### Provide ongoing support The engineering team will provide ongoing assistance to the partner during the development process, as needed to address any issues that may arise. ### Perform end-to-end testing with QA Once the partner completes their development, our QA team would assist in scheduling a one-hour testing session with your team. If any issues arise, they will be addressed by the engineering team. ### Production rollout The rollout for production will follow the same steps mentioned above, starting from setting up a client all the way to pushing catalogs. ## API endpoints The API operates in three countries, namely, UAE (Dubai), Jordan and KSA. ### Staging environment You can test your integration with the API in this environment. The base URL for the staging environment is: [https://apigateway-stg.careemdash.com/pos/api/v1](https://apigateway-stg.careemdash.com/pos/api/v1) #### Production environment The base URL for the production environment is: [https://apigateway.careemdash.com/pos/api/v1](https://apigateway.careemdash.com/pos/api/v1) ## Pagination API requests that return list of resources will be paginated to `20` resources by default. To navigate through the list of resources, you can use the `page_number` query parameter. You can also specify a different number of resources on each page by using the `page_size` parameter, with a maximum limit of 20. ## Rate limiting In order to prevent abuse and ensure overall system stability, the API implements rate limiting. We expect our partners to adhere to the following thresholds: - **Catalog API** - 1 catalog sync per branch every 2 minutes - 50 catalog syncs across all branches per minute - No more than 8,500 items can be provided in a catalog sync - **Item availability API** - No more than 40 items can be updated in a single API call You must apply necessary checks on your end to handle 429 (too many requests) status code from our APIs. ## Webhook availability Our API supports up to 4 retries for failed requests to the partner's webhook endpoint, with a maximum of 5 attempts in total, including the initial call. In case of the webhook endpoint being down, any event sent after the API exhausts its retries would be lost, making it crucial for partners to ensure its high availability.

get.description: Use this endpoint to fetch all branches that you've created.

parameters.name: page_size
parameters.description: Number of branches in one page

put.description:
Use this endpoint to create/update a branch.

A branch belongs to a brand, and when a branch is created through this API, it is not considered as a real branch in our system.
However, there is a manual process required by our operations team to map the branch to an actual Careem outlet.
This process must be completed only once after each new branch is created using this API.
You cannot create any catalog for a branch until it has mapped.

parameters.name: branch_id
parameters.description: A unique branch ID string provided by vendor or restaurant

409.description: Branch with the specified name already exists

get.description: Use this endpoint to fetch an existing branch by its ID.

404.description: Branch with the specified ID was not found

delete.description: Use this endpoint to delete an existing branch by its ID.

404.description: Branch with the specified ID was not found

patch.description:
Use this endpoint to enable or disable POS integration for a branch.

By default, this toggle is disabled when a branch is created.
This is to ensure that vendors can verify that the catalog syncs are successful before enabling the POS integration.
Once enabled, orders will be processed through the partner's POS system.
If the toggle is disabled, orders will not flow through the POS system.
Instead, they will be processed manually through the branch's tablets.
However, catalog syncs can still be made for the branch, even if the toggle is off.

parameters.name: branch_id
parameters.description: A unique branch ID string provided by vendor or restaurant

post.description:
Use this endpoint to update a branch's status on SuperApp.

A branch can hold one of following statuses:
 - `Active (1)`: Branch is active on the platform and customers are able to place orders.
 - `Inactive (2)`: Branch is active on the platform but customers would not be able to place orders.

Please be aware that it may take approximately 5 minutes for the changes to be reflected on the SuperApp.

parameters.name: branch_id
parameters.description: A unique branch ID string provided by vendor or restaurant

404.description: Either the brand or branch doesn't exist, or branch is not mapped

post.summary: Set branch status for a specified duration on SuperApp
post.description:
Use this endpoint to set a branch's status for a specified duration on SuperApp.

In the event of a busy kitchen or unforeseen circumstances that cause delays in order fulfillment, this API can be a useful solution.

parameters.name: branch_id
parameters.description: A unique branch ID string provided by vendor or restaurant

404.description: Either the brand or branch doesn't exist, or branch is not mapped

put.description: Use this endpoint to set a branch's operational hours.

get.description: Use this endpoint to fetch a branch's operational hours.

put.description:
Use this endpoint to create/update a catalog for a specific branch in one go.

The user can provide all necessary details and create/update the entire catalog with a single request.

Please be aware that it may take approximately 5 minutes for the changes to be reflected on the SuperApp.

delete.description:
Use this endpoint to reset/delete catalog for a branch.

This endpoint is **ONLY** meant to be used in case of data inconsistencies. Here is how you can carry this out in three steps:

1. Use this Delete API to reset/delete the catalog (calling this API will not immediately delete the catalog. Instead, the old catalog will ONLY be deleted when a new catalog request is sent with diff=false).
2. Push the entire catalog with diff=false.
3. If a catalog push request is in progress, then delete API would result in a 403 error code.

get.description: Use this endpoint to fetch the status of a catalog upload request by its ID.

parameters.name: request_id
parameters.description: The request ID string for the catalog uploaded. This is given as response for a create/update catalog request.

404.description: Catalog with the specified ID was not found

patch.description:
Use this endpoint to update the availability of a single or multiple items (maximum: 40) of a catalog.

Please be aware that it may take approximately 5 minutes for the changes to be reflected on the SuperApp.

parameters.name: catalog_id
parameters.description: A unique catalog ID string provided by vendor or restaurant

404.description: Catalog with the specified ID was not found

id.description: A unique item ID string provided by vendor or restaurant.

deleted.description: This field is used to delete item when only catalog difference is sent for updating.

priority.description: This field is used to specify the order in which this item should appear. The items with lower value are shown first. If this field is not sent then default priority will be 1 and ordering cannot be guaranteed.

tags.description: List of tags associated with the item. only predefined tags are allowed.

groups.description: All the IDs for the groups included in the request.

id.description: A unique category ID string provided by vendor or restaurant.

deleted.description: This field is used to delete category when only catalog difference is sent for updating.

priority.description: No two categories in a catalog can have same priority. This is a field which decides the order of categories shown in the mobile app. Categories with higher priority would be shown first. If this field is not sent then default priority will be 1 and ordering cannot be guaranteed.

sub_categories.description: All the IDs for the sub_categories included in the request.

items.description: All the IDs for the items included in the request.

id.description: A unique category ID string provided by vendor or restaurant.

deleted.description: This field is used to delete category when only catalog difference is sent for updating.

priority.description: No two categories in a catalog can have same priority. This is a field which decides the order of categories shown in the mobile app. Categories with higher priority would be shown first. If this field is not sent then default priority will be 1 and ordering cannot be guaranteed.

items.description: All the IDs for the items included in the request.

id.description: A unique group ID string provided by vendor or restaurant.

deleted.description: This field is used to delete group when only catalog difference is sent for updating.

multi_select.description:
This field is used to specify whether the options inside this group can be selected more than once.
The validation rules are as follows:

When multi_select is false:
  The group min must be between 0 and number of group options. (0 <= Group Min <= number of group options)
  The group max must be between the group min and number of group options. (Group Min <= Group Max <= number of group options)

When multi_select is true:
  The group min must be greater than 1. (Group Min > 1)
  The group max must be equal to the group min. (Group Max = Group Min)

Additionally, when the group object has nested groups, setting multi_select to true is not allowed.

min.description:
This field is used to specify the minimum number of options that can be selected from this group.
Corresponding Item will become unavailable if the number of available options in this group is less than the min value.

priority.description: This field is used to specify the order in which this group should appear. The groups with lower value are shown first. If this field is not sent then default priority will be 1 and ordering cannot be guaranteed.

options.description: All the IDs for the options included in the request.

id.description: A unique option ID string provided by vendor or restaurant.

deleted.description: This field is used to delete option when only catalog difference is sent for updating.

priority.description: This field is used to specify the order in which this option should appear. The options with lower value are shown first. If this field is not sent then default priority will be 1 and ordering cannot be guaranteed.

groups.description: All the IDs for the nested groups included in the request.

diff.description: If false, the whole catalog will be created or overridden if already created against the catalog id provided. The entities provided with IDs will be overridden or created. The entities missing from previous request will be deleted. If true, then only categories, items, options and groups provided will be added/updated as per the respective IDs. Deletion is not supported when diff is sent as true.

id.description: A unique string identifier for catalog provided by restaurant. Since, it is a unique string identifier, it can have values as "Default catalog" or "123006".

include_tax.description: true if the tax is already included in the items/options prices.

avg_price.description: The price is a float value.

currency_id.description:
The currency used for catalog. It is the integer ID corresponding to a currency. Allowed values are 1-14 with respective currency names and codes as follows:
```text | ID | Name | Code | |----|---------------------|------| | 1 | Dirham | AED | | 2 | Saudi Arabian Riyal | SAR | | 3 | Egyptian Pound | EGP | | 4 | Qatari Ria | QAR | | 5 | Lebanese pound | LBP | | 6 | Kuwaiti Dinar | KWD | | 7 | Jordanian Dinar | JOD | | 8 | Bahraini Dinar | BHD | | 9 | Singapore Dollar | SGD | | 10 | Iranian Rial | IRR | | 11 | Omani Rial | OMR | | 12 | Pakistani Rupee | PKR | | 13 | Australian Dollar | AUD | | 14 | Iraqi Dinar (IQD) | IQD | ```

order_instructions.description: Contains consistent and clear communication of merchant instructions, ensuring instructions are easily accessible and actionable.

tags.description: List of tags associated with the order, only predefined tags are allowed.

status.description: The current status of the order

cancellation_reason.description: The cancellation reason. This field is populated when the order is cancelled

original_total_price.description: is the total price of items and options without applying taxes nor discounts

tax_percentage.description: is the tax percentage of the order

total_taxable_price.description: is the total taxable amount of the order after applying taxes and the discount and adding delivery fees

free_delivery_discount_value.description: is the discount amount on delivery fee. This value does not affect the total price of the order. However, it is important for the partner to take this value into account when performing their calculations.

promo_code.description: Promotion code applicable for the order

Customer.description: information about the owner of the order in case of a self delivery orders

phone_number.description: the phone number of the customer

payment_type.description: the payment type chosen by the customer like cash, card etc

eta.description: is captain-to-merchant ETA in UTC using the RFC3339 format and is provided only in the pending event. This attribute will be absent if unavailable.

lat.description: the latitude of the address location

lng.description: the longitude of the address location

The state of the order.

We've recently introduced the `ready` state, which enables partners' to achieve more accurate tracking of their orders.

scopes.pos: Allows you to manage stores, menus and orders

tags.name: Auth overview
tags.description:
Our API uses OAuth2 and specifically **OAuth2 Client Credentials Grant** to authenticate incoming requests.

This is a general diagram of how API requests are authenticated.

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
  participant Client
  participant Careem Identity Provider
  participant Careem API

  Client->>Careem Identity Provider: Request access token
  Careem Identity Provider->>Careem Identity Provider: Authorize access token request
  Careem Identity Provider->>Client: Respond with access token
  Client->>Careem API: Request protected resource
  Careem API->>Careem API: Authenticate request with access token
  Careem API->>Client: Respond with protected resource
```

You may also find an overview of the flow [here.](https://www.oauth.com/oauth2-servers/access-tokens/client-credentials/)

tags.name: Auth endpoints
tags.description: This section covers endpoints related to authentication.

tags.name: Auth token
tags.description:
The token you receive from our identity provider is used to authenticate every request you send. The token should be embedded into the request's Authorization header with "Bearer" prefix.

# Scopes

Currently, all the available API endpoints support a single scope: pos

# Expiry

The token has an expiry of 24 hours, but you can renew the token any time you want by requesting a new token using the same flow.

# Renewal

The request of a new token does not invalidate the existing token(s). This means that if you have multiple services or multiple instances of the same service, they could all request the access token separately, so that you would not have to maintain a shared state between them. On the other hand, sharing the same access token between several services/instances is also fine.

In general, it is a good idea to not wait all the way till token expiry, but rather request a new token a bit in advance.

tags.name: Auth rate limiting
tags.description: Please store the received token on your side and do not try to request a new token every time you do an API request. This might potentially lead to unneeded rate-limiting or even IP block and might require manual intervention. If this does happen, please reach out to your Careem contact person.

tags.name: Store API overview
tags.description:
A brand is a unique identity that sets a company apart from its competitors. For example, KFC is a brand. Subway is a brand. Burger King is a brand too.

A branch is a physical outlet from which orders are picked up. For example, KFC located at Dubai Marina Mall is a branch. To create a branch, a brand must be created first.

There can be multiple branches associated with one brand but a branch can only be associated with one brand.

The **Store API** allows partners to create and manage their stores on Careem's platform.

tags.name: Store API components
tags.description:
- **List brands**
  - To view all the brands that you've created, use the GET /brands endpoint
- **Create a brand**
  - To create a brand, use the POST /brands endpoint
- **Fetch a brand**
  - To fetch an existing brand by its ID, use the GET /brands/{brand_id} endpoint
- **Update a brand**
  - Update a brand using the PUT /brands/{brand_id} endpoint
- **Delete a brand**
  - Delete a brand using the DELETE /brands/{brand_id} endpoint
- **List branches**
  - To view all the branches that you've created, use the GET /branches endpoint
- **Create a branch**
  - To create a branch, use the PUT /branches endpoint
- **Fetch a branch**
  - To fetch an existing branch by its ID, use the GET /branches/{branch_id} endpoint
- **Update a branch**
  - Update a branch using the PUT /branches/{branch_id} endpoint
- **Delete a branch**
  - Delete a branch using the DELETE /branches/{branch_id} endpoint
- **Toggle branch POS integration**
  - Use the PATCH /branches/{branch_id}/status endpoint to enable or disable POS integration for a branch. By default, this toggle is disabled when a branch gets created. Once enabled, orders will flow through partner's POS systems.
- **Update branch status on SuperApp**
  - Use the POST /branches/{branch_id}/visibility/status endpoint to update the branch status on SuperApp. If the status is set to active, the branch would appear on the SuperApp and customers would be able to place orders. However, if the status is set to inactive, the branch would appear on the SuperApp but customers would not be able to place orders.
  - Use the POST /branches/{branch_id}/visibility/status/expiries to set a branch's status to inactive for a limited period of time. Once the time passes, the branch would become active again.
- **Set and retrieve branch operational hours**
  - Set and retrieve operational hours for the branch via the PUT /operational-hours and GET /operational-hours endpoints.

tags.name: Store API operational hours
Store API endpoints
tags.description: This section covers endpoints for the store API.

tags.name: Store API flows
tags.description:
This section covers flows for the store API.

# Create brand

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
participant Client
participant Brand API
Client->>Brand API: Request to create a new brand via PUT /brands
Brand API->>Brand API: Verify uniqueness of the name
alt name is unique
Brand API->>Brand API: Create new brand
Brand API-->>Client: HTTP 200 OK
else name is in use
Brand API-->>Client: HTTP 409 Conflict
end
```

# Create branch

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
participant Client
participant Store API
Client->>Store API: Request to create a new branch via PUT /branches
Store API->>Store API: Verify uniqueness of the name
alt name is unique
Store API->>Store API: Create new branch
Store API-->>Client: HTTP 200 OK
else name is in use
Store API-->>Client: HTTP 409 Conflict
end
```

tags.name: Catalog API overview
tags.description:
A catalog represents the menu for a branch. It includes a hierarchical structure consisting of categories, subcategories, items, groups, and options.

- **Categories**
  - Classification of items based on a common trait.
  - An item can be associated with multiple categories.
  - A category is required to create items in a catalog.
  - For example, breakfast, appetizers and drinks are categories.
- **Subcategories**
  - A subcategory can be used for further division within a category.
  - A subcategory is optional when creating an item.
  - For example, the beverages category can be broken down into hot and cold items.
- **Items**
  - Product or dish in a restaurant.
  - For example, fried eggs in the breakfast category.
- **Groups**
  - Refers to the set of options/choices/add-ons/customizations.
  - The group is nested within an item, allowing customers to customize the item as needed.
  - For example, an option group can be 'Choose your Sauce' for the item 'Beef Burger'.
- **Options**
  - Represents options for a selected group.
  - For example, options can be Mushroom Sauce, BBQ Sauce, etc. for the group 'Choose your Sauce' under the item 'Beef Burger'.
- **Nested groups**
  - Identical to groups in their nature, but they are associated with options.
  - For example, a nested group can be 'Choose condiments' for the option 'French Fries' under the group 'Add a side item'.
- **Nested options**
  - Identical to options in their nature, but they are associated with nested groups.
  - For example, options can be Ketchup, Mayonnaise etc. for the nested group 'Choose condiments'.

The following visualization should give you a clear understanding of the hierarchy described above.

![Catalog hierarchy](/diagrams/hierarchy.svg)

A branch can have multiple catalogs associated with itself. However, only one catalog can be active at a given point in time.

The **Catalog API** allows partners to create and manage catalogs for their branch. The API also includes sending catalog sync notifications to a partner's webhook.

tags.name: Catalog API components
tags.description:
- **Upload catalog**
  - Upload catalog for a branch using the PUT /catalogs endpoint. This endpoint supports partial catalogs for subsequent invocations for a branch once its complete catalog has been uploaded.
- **Check catalog upload request status**
  - Use the GET /catalogs/status/{request_id} endpoint to fetch the status of a catalog upload request by its ID.
- **Update item(s) availability**
  - Update the availability of a single or multiple items (maximum: 40) of a catalog via the PATCH /catalogs/{catalog_id}/items endpoint.
- **Reset catalog**
  - Reset a catalog using the DELETE /catalogs endpoint. This endpoint is ONLY meant to be used in case of data inconsistencies.
- **Receive catalog sync notifications**
  - A catalog sync notification would be posted on your webhook in either of the following scenarios:
    - A catalog has successfully synced with our platform.
    - A catalog has failed to sync with our platform.

tags.name: Catalog API endpoints
tags.description: This section covers endpoints for the catalog API.

tags.name: Catalog API webhook events
tags.description:
The API supports the following webhook events:

| Event                  | Trigger                         |
|------------------------|---------------------------------|
| CATALOG_STATUS_UPDATED | A catalog request has processed |

tags.name: Catalog API flows
tags.description:
This section covers flows for the catalog API.

# Upload catalog

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
participant Client
participant Client webhook
participant Catalog API
Client->>Catalog API: Request to upload a new catalog via PUT /catalogs
Catalog API->>Catalog API: Verify mapping of branch
alt branch is mapped
Catalog API->>Catalog API: Verify amount of items in catalog
alt amount does not exceed threshold
Catalog API->>Catalog API: Verify if there is a running catalog upload for the branch
alt there is no running catalog upload
Catalog API->>Catalog API:  Upload catalog
Catalog API-->>Client: HTTP 200 OK
Catalog API-->>Client webhook: Post CATALOG_REQUEST_STATUS_UPDATED event
else there is already a running catalog upload
Catalog API-->>Client: HTTP 409 Conflict
end
else amount exceeds threshold
Catalog API-->>Client: HTTP 400 Bad Request
end
else branch is unmapped
Catalog API-->>Client: HTTP 400 Bad Request
end
opt fetch catalog upload request status
Client->Catalog API: Request to fetch catalog upload request status
Catalog API-->>Client: HTTP 200 OK
end
```

tags.name: Catalog API sample payloads
Order API overview
tags.description: The **Order API** allows partners to retrieve and manage orders. The API also includes sending order notifications to a partner's webhook.

tags.name: Order API components
tags.description:
- **List orders**
  - To access branch orders, use the GET /orders endpoint.
- **Fetch order details**
  - To fetch an order's details by its ID, use the GET /orders/{order_id} endpoint.
- **Accept/cancel order**
  - To accept/cancel an order, use the PUT /orders/{order_id} endpoint.
- **Mark order as ready**
  - To mark an order as ready for pickup, use the PUT /orders/{order_id} endpoint.
- **Tag orders**
  - Tag an order with your own metadata via the PATCH /orders/{order_id}/tags endpoint.
- **Receive order notifications**
    - The order API would send notifications to your webhook as an order progresses through different states.

tags.name: Order API endpoints
tags.description: This section covers endpoints for the order API.

tags.name: Order API webhook events
tags.description:
The API supports the following webhook events:

| Event                | Trigger                           |
|----------------------|-----------------------------------|
| ORDER_CREATED        | Customer has placed an order      |
| ORDER_STATUS_UPDATED | Order has advanced to a new state |

tags.name: Order API flows
tags.description:
This section covers flows for the order API.

# Order placement

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    actor Customer
    participant Careem SuperApp
    participant Client
    participant Client webhook
    participant Order API
    Customer->>Careem SuperApp: Customer places an order
    Careem SuperApp->>Order API: Receives the order with all details
    Order API-->>Client webhook: Sends an ORDER_CREATED event with status set as pending

    alt Partner accepts order
        Client->>Order API: Accepts the order via the PUT /orders/{order_id} endpoint
        Order API-->>Customer: Notify the customer that the order is being prepared
        Order API-->>Client webhook: Sends an ORDER_STATUS_UPDATED event with status set as accepted
    end

    alt Partner cancels order
        Client->Order API: Cancels the order via the PUT /orders/{order_id} endpoint
        Order API-->>Customer: Notify the customer that the order has been cancelled
        Order API-->>Client webhook: Sends an ORDER_STATUS_UPDATED event with status set as cancelled
    end

    opt Customer cancels order
        Customer->>Careem SuperApp: Customer cancels the order
        Careem SuperApp->>Order API: Cancel the order
        Order API-->>Customer: Notify the customer that the order has been cancelled
        Order API-->>Client webhook: Sends an ORDER_STATUS_UPDATED event with status set as cancelled
    end
```

tags.name: Order API pricing components
tags.description:
This section covers pricing components of an order.

# 1. Taxes

Tax is a percentage that is applied on the order total price.

# 2. Promotion Discounts

This is the first kind of discounts, and it is a percentage-based discount that is shared between Merchant and Careem. The discount is then applied to the total price of the order after taxes. It consists of two types:

1. `MerchantPromotion` is the discount amount (after calculating the percentage) that the Merchant provides to its customers
2. `CareemPromotion` is the discount amount (after calculating the percentage) that Careem provides to its customers

**Example:** The merchant has made the decision to offer a promotion discount of 10 AED. This decision was made after agreeing that Careem would contribute to 60% of the discount and the merchant would contribute to 40% of the discount. Therefore, `MerchantPromotion` is 4 AED and `CareemPromotion` is 6 AED.

# 3. Promo Code Discounts

This is the second kind of discounts, and it is the discount that is applied to specific orders which has a promo code applied by the customer on order check out. Promo code discount can be either percentage or fixed amount. Both Careem and Merchants can have different contributions in the same promo code discount.

**Example:** Promo Code `"FOODIE"` can give a fixed discount amount of 10 AED on the total order value. Careem contributes 60% and the merchant contributes 40% to the discount. So, Careem discount amount in this value is 6 AED and the merchant discount amount is 4 AED.

# 4. Delivery Fee

Delivery fee is a fixed amount that is added to the total price after calculating taxes and discounts.

# 5. Service Fee

Service fee is the customer fee which Careem collects from customers for overall service providing to customers from ordering to delivery. Service fee is collected on all orders and with all customers except for if there's a specific promo configured.

tags.name: Order API calculations
tags.description:
The standard formula for calculating total order price has been specified in the following: ```text $total_taxable_price = $sub_total_price + $delivery_fee + $service_fee ``` The delivery fee and service fee are quite straightforward. The following section focuses on the sub-total price. # Sub-total price To compute the sub-total price, you should perform the following steps: ## 1. Compute original/basket price This is computed by summing up the original prices of all items along with their options (without discounts). ```text $original_total_price = sum($items_original_prices) + sum($options_original_prices) ``` ## 2. Applying taxes if they exist The tax is then applied to the original/basket price by multiplying the tax rate. Please note that this step must be ignored if the tax is zero. ```text $sub_total_price_after_taxes = $original_total_price + ($original_total_price * tax_percentage) / 100 ``` ## 3. Applying the total promotion discount The total promotion discount is computed by adding Merchant's and Careem's contribution to the discount. ```text $total_promotion_discount = $merchant_discount_amount + $careem_discount_amount ``` It is then applied to the taxed price in the following manner: ```text $sub_total_price_after_taxes_and_promotions = $sub_total_price_after_taxes - $total_promotion_discount ``` ## 4. Applying the promocode discount The total promocode discount is computed by adding Merchant's and Careem's contribution to the promocode. ```text $total_promocode_discount = $merchant_promo_amount + $careem_promo_amount ``` It is then applied to the price computed in the above step in the following manner: ```text $sub_total_price = $sub_total_price_after_taxes_and_promotions - $total_promocode_discount ``` We can conclude this section as it represents the final step, where we have successfully computed the sub-total price. # Example Below is a sample request payload for an order:
Expand to see the payload ```json { "details": { "id": 100482992, "items": [ { "id": "UlVXLKDuEZ7tzQXxirYKYR0ZfOo", "notes": "test order", "groups": [ { "id": "I6YEPYTHjSAgMULUg5GXffZrPb4", "options": [ { "id": "2AbV6ggS8D6z234dCPz0CJ4Psyo", "quantity": 1, "total_price": 2.62 } ] }, { "id": "aLhJmZGI7ZbdS87TMr7I0cDgfh4", "options": [ { "id": "qkw-ui3UpUN6fhRBWFQGSCafnSo", "quantity": 1, "total_price": 1.05 }, { "id": "RKv1Bd-xp0i0jg0t_idGtcpdpGI", "quantity": 1, "total_price": 1.05 }, { "id": "T7jJ2ZEZscDgbrwr2h-H5lp2PEA", "quantity": 1, "total_price": 3.15 }, { "id": "4ioxDxPENssFA23turt4iYadaXY", "quantity": 1, "total_price": 2.1 } ] }, { "id": "i1gSIXvpL3j9kCcjWUaabiWQz1I", "options": [ { "id": "2KwZqe00QIDAQsvRi6si3Em3PmE", "quantity": 1, "total_price": 1.05 }, { "id": "lXHz6kvIpDEp8D2EtuB3EagYLgo", "quantity": 1, "total_price": 3.15 }, { "id": "2H7lPx-NILSNp6-3IzpoH6G4lSo", "quantity": 1, "total_price": 5.25 }, { "id": "FsUtWkstyiUIkwVvDwV0KEEoSIA", "quantity": 1, "total_price": 5.25 } ] } ], "quantity": 1, "unit_price": 10.5, "item_price": 10.5, "total_price": 35.17 }, { "id": "6ivLhXFVQqpXXyqrN_HIsaXdQTw", "notes": "", "groups": [], "discount": 4.2, "quantity": 2, "unit_price": 5.25, "item_price": 10.5, "total_price": 10.5 } ], "notes": "", "price": { "promo_code": "", "delivery_fee": 7, "free_delivery_discount_value": 0, "service_fee": 1, "tax_percentage": 0, "total_taxable_price": 35.4, "original_total_price": 46.67, "merchant_promo_amount": 0, "careem_promo_amount": 0, "merchant_discount_amount": 18.27, "careem_discount_amount": 0 }, "branch": { "id": "a5677341-8p06-4bf8-7b95-aaf42513569a", "name": "Example Branch", "state": "MAPPED", "created_at": "2020-10-28T15:05:48Z", "updated_at": "2020-11-04T08:51:01Z" }, "status": "pending", "captain": { "name": "", "phone_number": "" }, "cash_in": 0, "customer": { "name": "", "address": { "area": "", "city": "", "name": "", "note": "", "number": "", "street": "", "building": "", "location": { "lat": "", "lng": "" } }, "payment_type": "", "phone_number": 0 }, "created_at": "2020-11-25T08:57:35Z", "delivery_type": "careem", "merchant_pay_type": "", "cancellation_reason": "" }, "event_type": "ORDER_CREATED" } ```
## Givens - **Taxes:** 0% - **Merchant Promotion:** 18.27 - **Careem Promotion:** 0 - **Promo Code:** no promo code (Merchant contribution and Careem contribution are zero) - **Delivery Fee:** 7 - **Service Fee:** 1 ## Calculations ### 1. Calculate original/basket price ```text $original_total_price = sum($items_original_prices) + sum($options_original_prices) $item1_price = 10.5 $item2_price = 10.5 sum($items_original_prices) = $item1_price + $item2_price = 10.5 + 10.5 = 21 $item1_options_prices = 2.62 + 1.05 + 1.05 + 3.15 + 2.1 + 1.05 + 3.15 + 5.25 + 5.25 = 24.67 $item2_options_prices = 0 sum($options_original_prices) = $item1_options_prices + $item2_options_prices = 24.67 + 0 = 24.67 Let's plug in the values: $original_total_price = 21 + 24.67 = 45.67 ``` ### 2. Apply tax ```text $sub_total_price_after_taxes = $original_total_price + ($original_total_price * tax_percentage) / 100 $original_total_price = 45.67 $tax_percentage = 0 Let's plug in the values: $sub_total_price_after_taxes = 45.67 + (45.67 * 0) / 100 = 45.67 + 0 = 45.67 ``` ### 3. Apply total promotion discount ```text $total_promotion_discount = $merchant_discount_amount + $careem_discount_amount $merchant_discount_amount = 18.27 $careem_discount_amount = 0 Let's plug in the values to compute the total promotion discount: $total_promotion_discount = 18.27 + 0 = 18.27 Hence, $sub_total_price_after_taxes_and_promotions = $sub_total_price_after_taxes - $total_promotion_discount $sub_total_price_after_taxes_and_promotions = 45.67 - 18.27 = 27.40 ``` ### 4. Apply promocode discount ```text $total_promocode_discount = $merchant_promo_amount + $careem_promo_amount $merchant_promo_amount = 0 $careem_promo_amount = 0 Let's plug in the values to compute the total promocode discount: $total_promocode_discount = 0 + 0 = 0 Hence, $sub_total_price = $sub_total_price_after_taxes_and_promotions - $total_promocode_discount $sub_total_price = 27.40 - 0 = 27.40 ``` ### 5. Add delivery fee & service fee ```text $total_taxable_price = $sub_total_price + $delivery_fee + $service_fee $sub_total_price = 27.40 $delivery_fee = 7 $service_fee = 1 Let's plug in the values to get the total price: $total_taxable_price = 27.40 + 7 + 1 = 35.4 ``` # Order calculator You can utilize the provided calculator to observe the appearance of the pricing component within the webhook payload. It is pre-filled with the data from the above example.
Expand to view the calculator

tags.name: FAQs & Troubleshooting
tags.description:
This section covers commonly asked questions and issues that partners may encounter while using our API. If you have any questions or issues, feel free to contact us. We will do our best to assist you.

# API

**Question: I received the following API error. How should I resolve it?**
```json
{
  "message": "clients not found for client_id=<UUID>",
  "code": "NOT_FOUND_ERROR",
  "error_type": "NotFoundError",
  "errors": null
}
```

Before consuming our APIs, a webhook URL must be configured for the said environment. Please provide the webhook URL and the associated secret to the engineering team in order to resolve this issue.

**Question: I received the following API error. How should I resolve it?**

```json
{
  "message": "Validation Error",
  "code": "VALIDATION_ERROR",
  "error_type": "ValidationError",
  "errors": [
    {
      "errors": [
        {
          "message": "Name cannot be blank!"
        },
        {
          "message": "Name cannot be blank!"
        },
        {
          "message": "Name cannot be blank!"
        }
      ],
      "field": "Name"
    }
  ]
}
```

Please ensure entities such as brands, branches, catalogs, categories, items, groups and options have valid non-blank names in your request.

**Question: I received the following API error. How should I resolve it?**

```json
{
  "message": "Validation Error",
  "code": "VALIDATION_ERROR",
  "error_type": "ValidationError",
  "errors": [
    {
      "errors": [
        {
          "message": "branch_id is not mapped"
        }
      ],
      "field": "branch_id"
    }
  ]
}
```

This happens when a branch is not mapped. This is a necessary step for onboarding the branch to SuperApp. Please reach out to the operations team to help you out with the mapping.

**Question: I received the following API error. How should I resolve it?**

```json
{
  "message": "Forbidden Error",
  "code": "FORBIDDEN_ERROR",
  "error_type": "ForbiddenError",
  "errors": [
    {
      "reason": "Catalog cannot be reset for a branch with catalog creation in progress. Please wait until the job has ended."
    }
  ]
}
```

Please wait for the catalog to be processed on our end. If the processing doesn't complete within 30 minutes, please reach out to the engineering team.

**Question: I received the following error from the catalog reset API. How should I resolve it?**

```json
{
  "message": "Api deprecated Error",
  "code": "API_DEPRECATED_ERROR",
  "error_type": "APIDeprecatedError",
  "errors": null
}
```

This endpoint has been deprecated since 24th April 2024. Instead of catalog resets, we recommend all our partners to use the PUT /catalogs endpoint for catalog updates.

However, at the same time, you may request an approval for consuming the endpoint from your system.

**Question: I received a 401 error from the API. How should I resolve it?**

401 is returned when a valid token is not supplied with the request. Make sure that you've provided a valid token in your request. Please refer to the [authentication section](#tag/Auth-overview) for more details.

**Question: I received a 429 error from the API. How should I resolve it?**

429 means that you are being rate-limited due to a high number of requests. Please refer to the [Rate limiting section](#section/Getting-started/Rate-limiting) for more details.


# Webhook events

**Question: We have not received any order notification updates from Careem. How should I resolve this issue?**

This may happen if:
- The branch is not mapped to a Careem outlet. Contact operations team to map the branch.
- POS integration for the branch is disabled. Use API to enable POS integration.
- The branch is using Careem's portal to accept orders. Always log out and refrain from using the portal for accepting orders.

**Question: Are there any retries if the partner's webhook returns an HTTP status code other than 200 OK?**

Our API supports up to 4 retries for failed requests to the partner's webhook endpoint, with a maximum of 5 attempts in total, including the initial call.

**Question: Do you send any notification update on the partner's webhook when the branch's status is changed?**

No. We do not send any such updates to your webhook.

**Question: Why do I not get any customer information back on my webhook?**

Customer information is ONLY sent for self-delivery orders.

# Branch

**Question: Why is my order getting accepted immediately as soon as I place it?**

This happens if order auto-acceptance is enabled for your branch. Please reach out to our operations team to disable it.

**Question: Is there any time limit for order acceptance by a branch?**

Yes. This is configured by the Operations team.

**Question: The brand state shows as "UNMAPPED" while the branch that is created under the brand shows as "MAPPED". Is this normal?**

Yes, this is fine. The operations team maps branches ONLY, not brands.

**Question: Is the self-delivery model for branches supported by the API?**

Yes. Please refer to delivery_type in the order object

**Question: How do I persist my branch's location information?**

The API does not support any attribute for location. The location of the branch is populated by the operations team during the time of mapping.

**Question: Can Careem-based promotions and partner-based promotions run in parallel?**

Yes.

**Question: Is it possible to create discounts via the API?**

Currently, API does not support the creation of discounts. To process discounts and promotions, we follow the same procedures as we currently have in place. However, in the future, we plan to introduce APIs for these functions as well. The order details will include information on any discounts or partner shared promotions applied to the order, but the configuration of discounts and promotions is not possible through the APIs at this time.

**Question: Is it possible to support item-level discounts?**

No.

# Catalog

**Question: I received the following as the reason for my catalog failure. How should I resolve this issue?**
```text
validation error, customization group max must be between group min and number of group options when multiSelect is false
```

Please ensure that the group `min`, `max` and `multi_select` fields adhere to the following validation rules:

- When `multi_select` is false:
  - The group `min` must be between 0 and number of group options. (0 <= group `min` <= number of group options)
  - The group `max` must be between the group `min` and number of group options. (group `min` <= group `max` <= number of group options)
- When `multi_select` is true:
  - The group `min` must be greater than 1. (group `min` > 1)
  - The group `max` must be equal to the group min. (group `max` = Group `min`)
- Additionally, when the group object has nested groups, setting `multi_select` to true is not allowed.

**Question: What are the languages supported when sending a catalog?**

We only support Arabic and English at the moment.

**Question: What are the possible currencies?**

Please refer to the catalog.currency_id attribute in the catalog API response.

**Question: What is the order of precedence for the entire catalog in terms of operational hours?**

Catalog > Category > Sub-category > Item

**Question: Are all the prices of entities defined in the catalog tax-inclusive?**

Yes.

**Question: Can you please mention the VAT for KSA & UAE?**

KSA: 15%

UAE: 5%

# Order

**Question: What are the payment methods for captain and customer each?**

Refer to customer.payment_type for customer's payment methods in the order object.

Refer to merchant_pay_type for captain's payment methods in the order object.

**Question: During testing, we found out that the cash_in value is always coming as zero. How to identify the amount that the merchant will be collecting in self-delivery? Also, will it always be zero in case of Careem delivery?**

cash_in is the amount that needs to be collected from customers. If it's 0, you can assume the order is paid. And no, it would not always be 0.

**Question: What is merchant_pay_type?**

merchant_pay_type field refers to the way payment is exchanged between the merchant (restaurant) and the driver. It can be either "cash" or "prepaid"

**Question: Is merchant_pay_type of any significance to partners that use Careem delivery?**

For Careem delivery, merchant_pay_type is irrelevant because Careem collects the payments themselves.

For self-delivery, partners should look at customer.payment_type. That tells a partner whether it's a prepaid order or cash.

**Question: Are there any tracking capabilities for self-delivery orders? Is it possible for partners to push their order updates to your system?**

No. There are no tracking capabilities for self-delivery orders. The Careem SuperApp informs the user as such after placing the order.

**Question: Do integrated branches receive orders on the Careem dashboard?**

The restaurant's access to Careem dashboard will be restricted. They should immediately log out and use our partner's systems. All order statuses for integrated customers should be relayed via the API otherwise this could cause issues.

# SuperApp

**Question: How can I view the existing catalog of my branch?**

Unfortunately, we've deprecated the API endpoint for fetching catalogs. You can view the existing catalog by looking up your branch on SuperApp provided by our engineering team.

**Question: I am unable to find my branch on SuperApp. What could be the reason and how do I fix this?**

Please make sure that:

- Your location is set close to the branch's location.
- You've provided the correct name for the branch in the search bar.
- You've pushed a catalog and it has at least one active item.

**Question: How do I place an order from my branch on SuperApp, given that it is showing closed?**

Please enable POS integration for the branch via the branch API. If you have already done that, then please confirm the branch's operational hours.

If the time you choose to place your order falls outside the branch's operational hours, you will not be able to place an order. However, you can change the branch's operational hours via the API if you would like to.

**Question: Why am I not able to see the same name on SuperApp for my branch that I passed to the branch API?**

Please note that the name you see on SuperApp is decided mutually between you and the operations team. And this name could be different.

**Question: Do you display any calories information on SuperApp?**

No.

**Question: How much time does it take for the catalog to reflect on SuperApp?**

It usually takes around 5 minutes.

tags.description: This section covers the release notes for the API. For detailed release notes, please refer to [this document](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1JPQ1pKbKJSmZZsLGTBbH8R1JmlhRPeqP-EKqG5PXMVc/edit?usp=sharing).

post.description:
This notification is sent to the partner's webhook endpoint to indicate that a catalog request has been processed.
Note- Warnings may be included in the details if there are non-blocking issues with the catalog request. For example, for an item if mandatory option groups are ranked after optional groups.
warnings contain following information
- `entity_name` Name of the entity (category/item) that has the warning
- `entity_id` ID of the entity (category/item) that has the warning
- `entity_type` Type of the entity (CATEGORY/PRODUCT)
- `level` Level of the warning (Item/Category/Add-on group/Item)
- `severity` Severity of the warning (LOW/MEDIUM/HIGH)
- `description` Description of the warning
- `ticket_created_at` Time RFC3339 format in UTC when the warning ticket was created
- `entity_updated_at` Time RFC3339 format in UTC when the entity was last updated

warnings.description: Mandatory option groups are ranked after optional groups. Please fix the sorting

2XX.description: Return any 2XX status to indicate that the data was received successfully

post.description:
This notification is sent to the partner's webhook endpoint to indicate that a customer has placed an order on SuperApp.

Note: For scheduled orders, the value of `captain.eta` reflects the estimated time of arrival based on the customer's selected delivery time slot. 

The `delivery` field will include slot information exclusively for scheduled orders, providing details about the chosen time window.

The `Customer - segment` field indicates which segment a customer belongs to, based on their order history. This field can have one of the following values:

- New Customer:
    ```json
        "segments": [
          {
            "ar": "زبون جديد",
            "en": "New Customer"
          }
        ]
    ```
- Repeat Customer:
    ```json
        "segments": [
          {
            "ar": "زبون متكرر، ٢–٤ طلبات",
            "en": "Repeat Customer (2-4 Orders)"
          }
        ]
    ```
- High Value Customer
    ```json
        "segments": [
          {
            "ar": "عميل ذو قيمة عالية، أكثر من ٥ طلبات",
            "en": "High Value Customer (5+ Orders)"
          }
        ]
    ```

segments.ar: عميل ذو قيمة عالية، أكثر من ٥ طلبات

2XX.description: Return any 2XX status to indicate that the data was received successfully

post.description:
This notification is sent to the partner's webhook endpoint to indicate that an order has advanced to a new state.

All order states have been specified below:

| State         | Explanation                                                                                                                                                                               |
|---------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| accepted      | The order is now accepted by the branch and is being prepared.                                                                                                                            |
| slot_upcoming | For `scheduled orders`, this state is triggered a configurable number of minutes before the scheduled delivery slot begins. Partners can use this event to prepare for upcoming orders.   |
| slot_started  | For `scheduled orders`, this state is triggered at the exact start time of the scheduled delivery slot. Partners can use this event to begin preparing the order for pickup or delivery.  |
| driver_coming | When the captain has been assigned to the order and is on their way to the pickup location. The captain's details will be filled in the `captain` field of the order.                     |
| driver_here   | The captain has arrived at the pickup location and is ready to pick up the order.                                                                                                         |
| trip_started  | When the captain has picked up the order and is on their way to the drop-off location.                                                                                                    |
| trip_ended    | The order has been successfully delivered to the customer.                                                                                                                                |
| cancelled     | The order has been cancelled, and the reason for cancellation will be recorded in the `cancellation_reason` field of the order.                                                           |

Note: The key `pickup_time` will only be available when the order's state is in either `driver_coming` or `trip_started`.

The `delivery.delivery_mode` and `delivery.schedule_detail` fields are present only for scheduled orders, and will include the selected time slot information.

items.notes: Please don't ring the door bell

post.description:
This notification is sent to the partner's webhook endpoint to indicate that an item replacement request has been accepted by the customer.

modified item contains the `event` field with value `deleted` to indicate that the item has been removed from the order and `delta_quantity` field to indicate the number of items removed.

Note: At present event is available for `shops` only, and need merchant to suggest item replacement through careem's merchants portal/app.
